module_mood_contract: Prework is logistics, not a teaching beat. Must leave the engineer mood-ready for M1's 'joyful creation — it works, on my repo' opener: Claude Code open in chosen repo, curated skills installed, one trivial bug picked, Connections trick (or frustration) in hand. Most steps are SETUP — mood-exempt per check_strategy_tie_in §1 carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+ applies to TEACHING beats only; SETUP/LAUNCH beats mood-exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework body is 5 SETUP steps (pick repo, open session, fetch tarball, extract+install, screen+ready) plus a Connections-priming close. The only teaching-flavored beat is the close ('No trick? Bring a moment Claude Code frustrated you.') — scored 9. Setup steps judged on clarity+brevity, all clean.
No shame register anywhere. 'Your call' (L3, L36, L60), 'You can also download manually' (L44), 'Really stuck for a repo? Ask your trainer' (L30) all signal peer-trust, not correction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opening framing leads with arrival state ('Land at Module 1 with a Claude Code session started in the repo you're going to grow...' L7) — discipline-shaped, not 'don't show up unprepared'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc L270: 'Orientation is short, not a setup ramp. Prework has done Claude Code install + repo-choice + bug-surfacing. M1 starts in the chat with the repo open; Ex1 is a read-and-introspect move.' Prework delivers exactly that — install + repo + bug + skills install. Connections question is seeded as a teaser (L72), the framing 'lands live in Module 1' (L7) — no pre-explanation. Three-block memory, /context, MCP, plan mode, skill invocation: all absent. Clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three load-bearing forcing functions live in fenced registry markers (L42 ae101-prework-download-tarball, L50 ae101-prework-extract-and-install, L58 ae101-prework-screen-and-ready, L62 ae101-prework-one-at-a-time). Body callout at L56-L57 ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once. One bug at a time, then the next.') reads as a steering/recovery callout (per #62), not a forcing function the speed-reader needs.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversations or trainer cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework is self-study by design (read alone before M1). No 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'compare with the person next to you' shapes. Connections question at L72 names what the student brings to M1's opener — the room-harvest fires in M1, not here. Clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lead-ins above the four registry markers name the artifact/activity, not felt-evidence: L40 'Ask Claude to fetch ae101-content.tar.gz to ~/Downloads/' (artifact + destination); L48 'Mechanical setup. Claude needs to write outside the repo' (constraint + scope); L54 'Claude walks three trivial bug candidates from your repo's backlog, helps you pick one, then confirms the repo can test, branch, commit, and open a PR.' (activity sequence). No 'and you'll see how' / 'this reveals' shapes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework has no lectures; M1 is joyful-creation, not contrast-mood.

Per sim trace teaching_moment_assessment: the only teaching-flavored beat is the Connections close (L72). 'Bring a moment Claude Code frustrated you' as the no-trick fallback is the Godin move — pulls the close from functional to human, seeds M1's 'nobody taught you' Connections opener (getting-going.md L20) without front-running it.

Prework leaves the engineer mood-ready (Claude Code open, repo picked, skills installed, bug noted, trick or frustration chosen) without claiming any compound-loop ground. The 'first compound loop' framing lives in M1's Big Idea (getting-going.md L5), unreached here. M1's joyful-creation mood is set up by practical readiness + the Connections teaser — not pre-claimed.

Prework has no Big Idea section (logistics, not a module). Mood contract from strategy doc L270 ('Orientation is short, not a setup ramp') is honored — body is short, names the arrival state in one sentence (L7), and trusts the engineer to execute.

Prework close (L66-L72) hands directly to M1's open. (a) Bug-survives-to-M1 mechanic (L70) — M1 Ex2 fix-tests-first consumes the picked bug; artefact-contract row at L96 backs this. (b) 'Module 1 opens fresh in the same repo' (L68) matches M1's 'You'll open the first session in the repo you picked there.' (getting-going.md L11). (c) Connections question (L72) seeds M1's Connections opener verbatim shape (getting-going.md L20: 'what's one trick you figured out with Claude Code that nobody taught you?'). All three threads tight.
